rule.description: DLP: Generic data loss prevention event detected.
DLP: Bulk file copy from sensitive directory detected — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: Compression of files from sensitive directory — possible data staging for exfiltration.
DLP: Rclone cloud transfer detected — possible data exfiltration to cloud storage.
DLP: MEGA cloud tool usage detected — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: Cloud CLI upload detected — possible data exfiltration to cloud provider.
DLP: Command-line email with attachment — possible data exfiltration via email.
DLP: File copy to removable or non-system drive — potential data theft via USB.
DLP: New USB storage device registered — monitor for data exfiltration.
DLP: HTTP upload via command-line tool — possible data exfiltration over web.
DLP: File transfer via FTP/SCP/SFTP — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: Data piped through netcat/socat — possible covert exfiltration channel.
DLP: DNS tunneling tool detected — possible data exfiltration over DNS.
DLP: Search for sensitive data patterns — possible credential or PII harvesting.
DLP: Credential dumping tool detected — sensitive authentication data at risk.
DLP: Database export utility executed — possible bulk data exfiltration.
DLP: Steganography tool detected — possible covert data exfiltration via hidden data.
DLP: Screen capture tool executed — monitor for data exfiltration via screenshots.
DLP: Clipboard data access detected — possible sensitive data harvesting.
DLP: Data encoding or encryption before transfer — possible obfuscated exfiltration.
DLP: Sensitive file type created — potential data staging for exfiltration.
DLP: Non-root connection on file-transfer port — possible unauthorized data transfer.
DLP: Sensitive file downloaded from Office 365 — $(office_365.SourceFileName).
DLP: External sharing or anonymous link created in Office 365 — possible data exposure.
DLP: Exchange mail-flow (transport) rule modified — possible DLP policy bypass.
DLP: Email forwarding rule created in Office 365 — possible email exfiltration.
DLP: Access to sensitive AWS S3 bucket — monitor for data exfiltration.
DLP: AWS S3 bucket policy or ACL changed — possible data exposure risk.
DLP: High frequency bulk file copy operations — active data exfiltration in progress.
DLP: Repeated cloud transfer tool usage — sustained data exfiltration campaign.
DLP: Mass download of sensitive files from Office 365 — possible data theft.
DLP: Sysmon outbound FTP/TFTP connection by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible file exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon outbound connection on suspicious port $(win.eventdata.destinationPort) by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible covert exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon cloud/messaging app network connection by $(win.eventdata.image) — monitor for data exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon scripting engine $(win.eventdata.image) initiating outbound HTTP/S to $(win.eventdata.destinationIp) — possible script-based exfiltration.
DLP: High-frequency scripting engine outbound connections — active data exfiltration campaign via scripts.
DLP: Sysmon archive file created in temp directory by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible data staging.
DLP: Sysmon sensitive file type created in staging directory by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible data collection.
DLP: Sysmon scripting engine created archive/encrypted file — possible automated data packaging for exfiltration.
DLP: Mass sensitive file creation in staging directory — active data collection in progress.
DLP: Sysmon LSASS memory access by $(win.eventdata.sourceImage) — credential theft risk detected.
DLP: Sysmon process access to $(win.eventdata.targetImage) from suspicious path — possible credential harvesting.
DLP: Sysmon Zone.Identifier ADS written by scripting engine $(win.eventdata.image) — possible downloaded content manipulation.
DLP: Sysmon non-standard Alternate Data Stream created by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible covert data hiding.
DLP: Sysmon DNS lookup for file-sharing service $(win.eventdata.queryName) by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible data exfiltration channel.
DLP: Sysmon DNS query with unusually long subdomain by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible DNS tunneling exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon DNS query for dark web domain $(win.eventdata.queryName) — possible covert data exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon DNS query by cloud/messaging app $(win.eventdata.image) — monitor for data exfiltration.
DLP: Burst of DNS queries with long subdomains — active DNS tunneling exfiltration detected.
DLP: Sysmon archive/encrypted file deleted from temp by $(win.eventdata.image) — possible post-exfiltration cleanup.
DLP: Sysmon secure deletion tool $(win.eventdata.image) used — possible evidence destruction after exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon registry change to removable storage policy — possible DLP bypass attempt.
DLP: Sysmon Windows Defender policy modified — possible security control bypass to facilitate exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon data collection policy registry key modified — possible audit evasion.
DLP: Sysmon file-transfer tool $(win.eventdata.image) launched from $(win.eventdata.parentImage) — possible user-initiated exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon executable from temp/downloads path with exfiltration keywords — possible staging tool execution.
DLP: Sysmon PowerShell Compress-Archive targeting sensitive directory — possible data staging.
DLP: Sysmon PowerShell data export to staging directory — possible data collection for exfiltration.
DLP: Sysmon recursive file enumeration piped to copy/archive — possible bulk data collection.
DLP: Sysmon HTTP file upload via PowerShell/curl — active data exfiltration attempt.
DLP: Linux bulk file copy from sensitive directory — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: Linux compression of sensitive directory — possible data staging for exfiltration.
DLP: Linux rclone cloud transfer detected — possible data exfiltration to cloud storage.
DLP: Linux MEGA cloud tool usage detected — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: Linux cloud CLI upload detected — possible data exfiltration to cloud provider.
DLP: Linux DNS tunneling tool detected — possible data exfiltration over DNS.
DLP: Linux credential or auth file access — possible credential harvesting.
DLP: Linux credential dumping tool detected — sensitive authentication data at risk.
DLP: Linux database export utility executed — possible bulk data exfiltration.
DLP: Linux HTTP file upload via curl/wget — possible data exfiltration over web.
DLP: Linux data piped through netcat/socat — possible covert exfiltration channel.
DLP: Linux file transfer to remote host via SCP/SFTP/rsync — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: Linux screen capture tool executed — monitor for data exfiltration via screenshots.
DLP: Linux clipboard data access detected — possible sensitive data harvesting.
DLP: Linux data encoding or encryption before transfer — possible obfuscated exfiltration.
DLP: Linux search for sensitive data patterns — possible credential or PII harvesting.
DLP: Linux secure deletion tool used — possible evidence destruction after exfiltration.
DLP: Linux command-line email with attachment — possible data exfiltration via email.
DLP: Linux steganography tool detected — possible covert data exfiltration via hidden data.
DLP: Linux recursive search for sensitive file types — possible data discovery for exfiltration.
DLP: Linux outbound connection on suspicious port $(eventdata.destinationPort) by $(eventdata.image) — possible covert exfiltration.
DLP: Linux scripting engine $(eventdata.image) initiating outbound HTTP/S — possible script-based exfiltration.
DLP: Linux cloud/messaging app network connection by $(eventdata.image) — monitor for data exfiltration.
DLP: Linux archive file created in temp directory by $(eventdata.image) — possible data staging.
DLP: Linux sensitive file type created in staging directory by $(eventdata.image) — possible data collection.
DLP: Linux archive/encrypted file deleted from temp by $(eventdata.image) — possible post-exfiltration cleanup.
DLP: High frequency Linux bulk file copy operations — active data exfiltration in progress.
DLP: Repeated Linux cloud transfer tool usage — sustained data exfiltration campaign.
DLP: High-frequency Linux scripting engine outbound connections — active script-based exfiltration.
DLP: Mass sensitive file creation in Linux staging directory — active data collection in progress.
DLP: macOS bulk file copy from user directory — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: macOS compression of user directory — possible data staging for exfiltration.
DLP: macOS disk image creation from user data — possible data staging via .dmg.
DLP: macOS Keychain credential access — possible credential harvesting.
DLP: macOS screen capture executed — monitor for data exfiltration via screenshots.
DLP: macOS clipboard data access detected — possible sensitive data harvesting.
DLP: macOS AppleScript with network or shell activity — possible script-based exfiltration.
DLP: macOS HTTP file upload via curl/wget — possible data exfiltration over web.
DLP: macOS file transfer to remote host via SCP/SFTP/rsync — possible data exfiltration.
DLP: macOS rclone cloud transfer detected — possible data exfiltration to cloud storage.
DLP: macOS data encoding or encryption before transfer — possible obfuscated exfiltration.
DLP: macOS search for sensitive data patterns — possible credential or PII harvesting.
DLP: macOS recursive search for sensitive file types — possible data discovery for exfiltration.
DLP: macOS database export utility executed — possible bulk data exfiltration.
DLP: macOS secure deletion tool used — possible evidence destruction after exfiltration.
DLP: macOS outbound connection on suspicious port $(eventdata.destinationPort) by $(eventdata.image) — possible covert exfiltration.
DLP: macOS scripting engine $(eventdata.image) initiating outbound HTTP/S — possible script-based exfiltration.
DLP: macOS archive/disk image created in temp directory by $(eventdata.image) — possible data staging.
DLP: macOS sensitive file type created in staging directory by $(eventdata.image) — possible data collection.
DLP: macOS archive/encrypted file deleted from temp by $(eventdata.image) — possible post-exfiltration cleanup.
DLP: High frequency macOS bulk file copy operations — active data exfiltration in progress.
DLP: Repeated macOS cloud transfer tool usage — sustained data exfiltration campaign.
DLP: High-frequency macOS scripting engine outbound connections — active script-based exfiltration.
DLP: Mass sensitive file creation in macOS staging directory — active data collection in progress.
